Scenario.overview: Water and Wastewater Systems Simple A basic-level cyber attack targeting water and wastewater systems begins with phishing attempts against internet-facing assets, leading to the execution of malicious files via reg.exe utility, ultimately resulting in data manipulation impacting system operations.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, File System, OS Process
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method, Reg.exe Utility, Data Manipulation

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: An attacker sends phishing emails to employees, tricking them into clicking on malicious links or opening infected attachments which leads to initial access.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2016-1555: NETGEAR Wireless AP Command Injection
stage.capec: CAPEC-635: Alternative Execution Due to Deceptive Filenames
stage.description: Upon successful phishing, the malicious attachment is executed using the reg.exe utility, allowing the attacker to run arbitrary commands on the compromised system.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker manipulates data within the system processes to disrupt operations, potentially causing operational failures or safety hazards within the water and wastewater systems.

Scenario.summary: This simple cyber attack involves phishing to gain initial access, followed by executing malicious files to manipulate critical system data, thereby disrupting the operations of water and wastewater systems.
